MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

CueMix FX Settings

Sample Rate

Set the sample rate in CueMix FX (or MOTU Audio Console) before opening your Ableton session. The MOTU clock drives Ableton — both must agree on sample rate.

  • 44.1 kHz: standard music production. Best DAW plugin compatibility. Lowest file sizes and CPU load.
  • 48 kHz: use when the project will be synced to video or exported for video post-production.
  • 96 kHz: recommended for high-resolution recording of the Hydrasynth — captures upper harmonics from wavetable morphing. 2× the data rate of 44.1 kHz.
  • 192 kHz: mastering and archival use only; reduces available I/O channel count on the MOTU.

Buffer Size and Latency

Buffer SizeApprox. Latency @ 44.1 kHzRecommended For
64 samples~1.5 msLive performance, real-time monitoring
128 samples~3 msRecording with musicians
256 samples~5.8 msGeneral Ableton sessions
512 samples~11.6 msMixing with heavy plugin loads

Start at 256 samples for production work. Reduce to 128 when tracking live instruments or vocals. Drop to 64 only when performing live alongside a drummer or other locked-tempo musicians where even 3 ms matters.

Clock Source

Set to Internal in almost all cases. The MOTU generates its own stable word clock and Ableton slaves to it via the ASIO driver. Only switch to S/PDIF or an external clock source if a dedicated external clock generator is present in the studio.

Latency-Free Monitoring

This is CueMix's biggest advantage. When recording a vocalist through the SM58:

  1. In CueMix, route the SM58 input (Input 1) directly to the headphone output bus (Mix 2).
  2. In Ableton, set the tracking Audio Track's Monitor to Off. This prevents the software-path signal from doubling with the CueMix direct monitor.
  3. The vocalist hears themselves with zero perceptible delay — the signal never touches the computer in the monitoring path.

Saving CueMix Scenes

Use File → Save As to save complete CueMix FX configurations. Create named scenes for: Tracking (minimal reverb, direct mic monitoring), Production (full bus routing), and Live (standalone mixer layout). Recall any scene in seconds at the start of a session.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
